articles.headline: India Launches National Critical Minerals Mission to Secure Supply Chains for Green Transition
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the establishment of the National Critical Minerals Mission (NCMM) aimed at securing and processing essential minerals for India's energy transition and high-tech manufacturing.
• The mission focuses on identifying domestic reserves, promoting sustainable mining, and forging international partnerships for critical mineral acquisition.
• It includes provisions for incentivizing private sector participation in exploration, mining, and beneficiation of minerals like lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements.
• A dedicated fund will be established to support research and development in critical mineral processing technologies and recycling.
• This initiative is crucial for achieving India's net-zero targets and reducing reliance on imports for strategic sectors.

articles.trivia: Did you know that lithium, crucial for EV batteries, is not currently mined in significant quantities in India, making secure international supply chains vital?

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Government's Push for Digitization of Land Records, Citing Transparency and Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court dismissed a batch of petitions challenging the constitutionality of a central government scheme promoting the digitization of land records across states.
• The court emphasized that digitizing land records is a crucial step towards ensuring transparency, reducing land disputes, and improving governance.
• It directed state governments to accelerate the implementation of the Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) while ensuring data security and privacy safeguards.
• The ruling is expected to boost efforts to create a unified and accessible land information system, benefiting farmers and property owners.
• Concerns raised about data security and potential exclusion were acknowledged, with the court stressing robust mechanisms for grievance redressal.

articles.trivia: The DILRMP scheme was launched in 2008 by merging two existing schemes: the Computerization of Land Records (CLR) and the Strengthening of Revenue Administration and Updating of Land Records (SRA&ULR).

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Joint Declaration on Maritime Security and Digital Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded in Jakarta, with leaders adopting a joint declaration focused on enhancing cooperation in maritime security, digital transformation, and green economy.
• Prime Minister of India reiterated India's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and emphasized the importance of multilateralism.
• Key outcomes include agreements on joint naval exercises, collaboration on cybersecurity infrastructure, and initiatives for renewable energy technology transfer.
• The summit also saw discussions on regional connectivity projects and strengthening supply chain resilience.
• This declaration solidifies the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership between India and ASEAN, addressing contemporary regional and global challenges.

articles.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision comes as retail inflation remains above the MPC's comfort zone, driven by volatile food prices and elevated core inflation.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic slowdowns and geopolitical tensions as key risks to domestic growth and inflation outlook.
• The central bank reaffirmed its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to bring inflation within the target range while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with future rate cuts dependent on a sustained decline in inflation and stable global conditions.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

articles.headline: Mid-Term Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Reveals Mixed Progress
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its mid-term review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting both successes and areas needing accelerated action.
• Significant progress was noted in foundational literacy and numeracy initiatives, teacher training programs, and the integration of vocational education.
• Challenges persist in achieving universal access to quality early childhood care and education, reforming assessment patterns, and ensuring adequate digital infrastructure in rural areas.
• The report recommends increased budgetary allocation for education, enhanced public-private partnerships, and stronger state-level monitoring mechanisms.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic development and multidisciplinary learning.

articles.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Talks Enter Critical Phase, Focus on Services and Investment
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered a crucial final phase, with both sides intensifying discussions on key contentious issues.
• The focus has shifted to market access for services, particularly in financial, legal, and IT sectors, and investment protection clauses.
• India is pushing for greater mobility for its professionals, while the UK seeks tariff reductions on specific goods and intellectual property rights protection.
• A breakthrough is anticipated by year-end, potentially unlocking significant trade and investment opportunities for both nations.
• The FTA aims to double bilateral trade by 2030, strengthening economic ties post-Brexit for the UK and diversifying trade partners for India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Landing Experiment for Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved another milestone by successfully conducting a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The test involved a scaled-down RLV prototype being released from an aerial platform and performing a precise autonomous landing on a designated runway.
• This experiment validates key technologies such as autonomous navigation, guidance, and control systems essential for future winged reusable launchers.
• Reusable launch vehicles are expected to significantly reduce the cost of space access, making space missions more frequent and economical.
• ISRO aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV capable of vertical take-off and horizontal landing, similar to space shuttles.

articles.headline: Ministry of Urban Development Unveils New Policy to Boost Urban Green Spaces and Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Urban Development launched a comprehensive new policy aimed at increasing urban green spaces, promoting biodiversity, and enhancing the ecological health of Indian cities.
• The policy mandates minimum per capita green cover for urban areas, encourages the development of urban forests, parks, and green corridors, and integrates nature-based solutions into urban planning.
• It emphasizes the protection of existing wetlands and water bodies within city limits and promotes sustainable landscaping practices.
• Funding mechanisms and technical assistance will be provided to urban local bodies for implementing green infrastructure projects.
• The initiative seeks to improve air quality, mitigate urban heat island effects, and enhance the overall quality of life for city dwellers.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Enhance Joint Maritime Patrols to Counter Smuggling and Illegal Fishing
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and the Sri Lanka Navy commenced an intensified series of coordinated joint maritime patrols in the Palk Strait and Gulf of Mannar.
• The initiative aims to curb illegal fishing activities, drug trafficking, and other illicit maritime operations plaguing the shared waters.
• This enhanced cooperation follows recent bilateral discussions focusing on strengthening maritime security and addressing humanitarian concerns of fishermen.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to upholding international maritime law and ensuring the safety and security of the sea lanes.
• The patrols are expected to improve real-time information sharing and operational coordination between the two navies.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National AI Strategy Framework for Ethical Development and Deployment
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released India's National AI Strategy Framework, focusing on ethical AI development, data governance, and fostering innovation.
• The framework outlines guidelines for responsible AI, emphasizing principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy in AI systems.
• It proposes establishing a National AI Council and sector-specific AI centers of excellence to drive research and application across various domains.
• The strategy aims to position India as a global hub for AI innovation while ensuring that AI benefits all sections of society and addresses national priorities.
• Key focus areas include AI for healthcare, agriculture, smart cities, and education, alongside skill development programs for the AI workforce.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase II Shows Promising Results in River Clean-up and Ecosystem Restoration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti reported significant progress in the second phase of the Namami Gange Mission, focusing on comprehensive river basin management.
• Achievements include the commissioning of several new sewage treatment plants (STPs), reduction in industrial pollution discharge, and enhanced ecological flow in the Ganges.
• Efforts have also been made in afforestation along the river banks, promoting organic farming in the basin, and community engagement for river conservation.
• Challenges remain in ensuring continuous operation and maintenance of infrastructure, managing non-point source pollution, and behavioral change among communities.
• The mission aims for 'Aviral and Nirmal Ganga' (uninterrupted and clean Ganga) by 2027, integrating conservation with economic development.

Disaster and disaster management.

articles.trivia: The Namami Gange Programme was launched in 2014 as an integrated conservation mission to accomplish the twin objectives of effective abatement of pollution, conservation, and rejuvenation of the National River Ganga.

articles.headline: Cabinet Approves New PLI Scheme to Boost Advanced Textile Manufacturing and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for the advanced textile sector, including technical textiles and MMF (Man-Made Fibre) apparel.
• The scheme aims to attract significant investment, create employment, and enhance India's manufacturing capabilities in high-value textile products.
• Incentives will be provided for eligible manufacturers based on incremental sales and investment in specified product segments.
• This move is designed to make India globally competitive in textile manufacturing, reduce import dependence, and boost exports.
• The textile sector is a major employer, and this scheme is expected to drive modernization and technological upgrades, contributing to economic growth.

articles.headline: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports Launches Policy to Promote Indigenous Sports and Traditional Games
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports introduced a new national policy aimed at recognizing, preserving, and promoting India's rich heritage of indigenous sports and traditional games.
• The policy includes provisions for financial assistance to traditional sports federations, establishment of academies for training, and inclusion of select indigenous games in national-level competitions.
• It emphasizes the cultural significance of games like Kho Kho, Kabaddi, Mallakhamb, and Gatka, and their potential to foster physical fitness and cultural identity.
• Educational institutions will be encouraged to incorporate these games into their curriculum and extracurricular activities.
• The initiative seeks to revive fading traditional sports, create new employment opportunities, and enhance India's soft power globally.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Sustainable Tourism for Future' Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled its 'Sustainable Tourism for Future' policy, aiming to promote environmentally responsible and culturally sensitive tourism practices across India.
• The policy focuses on minimizing the negative environmental impact of tourism, preserving local cultures, and ensuring economic benefits for host communities.
• It includes guidelines for eco-tourism development, waste management in tourist destinations, promotion of local handicrafts, and responsible wildlife tourism.
• Incentives will be provided for tourism operators and states adopting sustainable certification standards and green technologies.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to the UN Sustainable Development Goals and seeks to reposition India as a leading sustainable tourism destination globally.

articles.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions Amidst Climate Change Concerns
articles.explainer:
• India has formally initiated discussions with Pakistan to review certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), citing concerns over the impact of climate change on river flows and water availability.
• The move comes after India's repeated calls for Pakistan to engage in technical discussions on the treaty's interpretation and implementation, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects.
• India argues that the existing provisions, drafted in 1960, do not adequately account for modern hydrological data and the effects of global warming on glacial melt and precipitation patterns.
• Pakistan, while acknowledging the discussions, has reiterated its commitment to the treaty's integrity and called for adherence to its dispute resolution mechanisms.
• The IWT allocates the waters of the Indus, Jhelum, Chenab (Western Rivers) to Pakistan and Ravi, Beas, Sutlej (Eastern Rivers) to India.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.

Eastern Rivers: Ravi, Beas, Sutlej (allocated to India).
Year treaty signed: 1960.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and significance of the Indus Waters Treaty for India-Pakistan relations.

articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was brokered by the World Bank and is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: National Health Authority Reports Significant Progress in Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Adoption
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved significant milestones, with over 500 million health IDs created and a rapid increase in health facility registrations.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare, enabling interoperability of health data across the country.
• Key successes include enhanced digital health record linkages, teleconsultation services, and increasing adoption by private healthcare providers.
• Challenges persist in ensuring universal digital literacy, addressing data privacy concerns, and integrating all legacy health systems.
• ABDM is a cornerstone of India's digital health ecosystem, promising improved access, efficiency, and quality of healthcare services.

articles.headline: Bharat Mandapam Set to Host Major International Climate Finance Summit
articles.explainer:
• The newly inaugurated Bharat Mandapam, International Exhibition-cum-Convention Centre in New Delhi, has been selected to host a significant international summit on climate finance later this year.
• The summit will bring together global leaders, financial institutions, and climate experts to discuss strategies for mobilizing funds for climate action and green initiatives in developing nations.
• India's selection as the host reflects its growing leadership in climate diplomacy and its commitment to sustainable development goals.
• The event is expected to facilitate discussions on innovative financing mechanisms, technology transfer, and capacity building for climate resilience.
• Hosting such a high-profile event further solidifies Bharat Mandapam's role as a premier venue for global conferences.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in the Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• The newly identified amphibian, characterized by unique vocalizations and distinct morphological features, highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region.
• The discovery underscores the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from habitat loss and climate change.
• Researchers employed advanced genetic sequencing and bioacoustics to confirm the species' novelty, adding to the growing list of endemic species in the region.
• This finding is expected to bolster ecological studies and conservation strategies for amphibians, which are often indicators of environmental health.

articles.headline: Government Initiatives Boost Millet Production and Consumption for Enhanced Food Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare highlighted the success of various government initiatives aimed at promoting millet cultivation and consumption across India.
• These initiatives include financial support to farmers, development of drought-resistant millet varieties, and public awareness campaigns about the nutritional benefits of millets.
• The push for millets aligns with the 'International Year of Millets 2023' momentum and India's commitment to diversifying food baskets for better nutrition and climate resilience.
• Millets are recognized for their high nutritional value, low water requirement, and ability to thrive in arid conditions, making them ideal for sustainable agriculture.
• Efforts are underway to integrate millets into public distribution systems and mid-day meal schemes to combat malnutrition.

articles.headline: MeitY Issues Updated Guidelines for Online Gaming Industry, Emphasizing Player Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released updated guidelines for the online gaming industry, focusing on enhanced player protection, responsible gaming, and transparent operations.
• The guidelines mandate stricter Know Your Customer (KYC) norms, age verification for players, and a robust grievance redressal mechanism for online gaming platforms.
• They also introduce provisions for self-regulatory bodies (SRBs) to certify permissible online games and ensure compliance with the new framework.
• The policy aims to foster a safe and responsible online gaming ecosystem while promoting innovation and growth in the sector.
• Concerns regarding addiction, financial fraud, and data privacy were central to the formulation of these revised rules, seeking to balance regulation with industry development.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Critical Minerals Mission (NCMM), consider the following statements:
1. Its primary objective is to secure critical minerals for India's energy transition.
2. It focuses exclusively on domestic exploration and mining of these minerals.
3. Lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements are among the key minerals targeted by the mission.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The NCMM aims to secure critical minerals for India's energy transition and high-tech manufacturing. Statement 2 is incorrect. While it focuses on domestic reserves, it also includes forging international partnerships for critical mineral acquisition. Statement 3 is correct. Lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements are explicitly mentioned as key minerals.

prelimsMocks.question: The Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) is primarily associated with which of the following objectives?
options: (a) Providing digital literacy to rural populations.
(b) Digitization of land records to enhance transparency and reduce disputes.
(c) Promoting e-commerce platforms for agricultural produce.
(d) Developing smart city infrastructure through digital mapping.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Digitization of land records to enhance transparency and reduce disputes.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program, what is the primary benefit of developing such technology?
options: (a) To launch heavier satellites into geosynchronous orbit.

(c) To enable human spaceflight missions to Mars.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To significantly reduce the cost of space access.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that 'Reusable launch vehicles are expected to significantly reduce the cost of space access, making space missions more frequent and economical.' While other options might be future goals of space exploration, cost reduction is the immediate and primary benefit of RLV technology.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports Launches Policy to Promote Indigenous Sports and Traditional Games
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports introduced a new national policy aimed at recognizing, preserving, and promoting India's rich heritage of indigenous sports and traditional games.
• The policy includes provisions for financial assistance to traditional sports federations, establishment of academies for training, and inclusion of select indigenous games in national-level competitions.
• It emphasizes the cultural significance of games like Kho Kho, Kabaddi, Mallakhamb, and Gatka, and their potential to foster physical fitness and cultural identity.
• Educational institutions will be encouraged to incorporate these games into their curriculum and extracurricular activities.
• The initiative seeks to revive fading traditional sports, create new employment opportunities, and enhance India's soft power globally.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Government's Push for Digitization of Land Records, Citing Transparency and Efficiency

• The court emphasized that digitizing land records is a crucial step towards ensuring transparency, reducing land disputes, and improving governance.
• It directed state governments to accelerate the implementation of the Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) while ensuring data security and privacy safeguards.
• The ruling is expected to boost efforts to create a unified and accessible land information system, benefiting farmers and property owners.
• Concerns raised about data security and potential exclusion were acknowledged, with the court stressing robust mechanisms for grievance redressal.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions Amidst Climate Change Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• India has formally initiated discussions with Pakistan to review certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), citing concerns over the impact of climate change on river flows and water availability.
• The move comes after India's repeated calls for Pakistan to engage in technical discussions on the treaty's interpretation and implementation, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects.
• India argues that the existing provisions, drafted in 1960, do not adequately account for modern hydrological data and the effects of global warming on glacial melt and precipitation patterns.
• Pakistan, while acknowledging the discussions, has reiterated its commitment to the treaty's integrity and called for adherence to its dispute resolution mechanisms.
• The IWT allocates the waters of the Indus, Jhelum, Chenab (Western Rivers) to Pakistan and Ravi, Beas, Sutlej (Eastern Rivers) to India.

GS3.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision comes as retail inflation remains above the MPC's comfort zone, driven by volatile food prices and elevated core inflation.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic slowdowns and geopolitical tensions as key risks to domestic growth and inflation outlook.
• The central bank reaffirmed its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to bring inflation within the target range while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with future rate cuts dependent on a sustained decline in inflation and stable global conditions.

Explain the tools used by the RBI under its monetary policy framework.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Landing Experiment for Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved another milestone by successfully conducting a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The test involved a scaled-down RLV prototype being released from an aerial platform and performing a precise autonomous landing on a designated runway.
• This experiment validates key technologies such as autonomous navigation, guidance, and control systems essential for future winged reusable launchers.
• Reusable launch vehicles are expected to significantly reduce the cost of space access, making space missions more frequent and economical.
• ISRO aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV capable of vertical take-off and horizontal landing, similar to space shuttles.

GS3.imageDescription: An artist's impression of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle making a horizontal landing on a runway.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), was successfully conducted in 2016, demonstrating the hypersonic flight capabilities of the winged body.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Urban Development Unveils New Policy to Boost Urban Green Spaces and Biodiversity
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Urban Development launched a comprehensive new policy aimed at increasing urban green spaces, promoting biodiversity, and enhancing the ecological health of Indian cities.
• The policy mandates minimum per capita green cover for urban areas, encourages the development of urban forests, parks, and green corridors, and integrates nature-based solutions into urban planning.
• It emphasizes the protection of existing wetlands and water bodies within city limits and promotes sustainable landscaping practices.
• Funding mechanisms and technical assistance will be provided to urban local bodies for implementing green infrastructure projects.
• The initiative seeks to improve air quality, mitigate urban heat island effects, and enhance the overall quality of life for city dwellers.

GS3.trivia: The 'Miyawaki method' is a technique for building dense, native forests quickly, often used in urban areas to rapidly increase green cover.

GS3.headline: India and Sri Lanka Enhance Joint Maritime Patrols to Counter Smuggling and Illegal Fishing
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and the Sri Lanka Navy commenced an intensified series of coordinated joint maritime patrols in the Palk Strait and Gulf of Mannar.
• The initiative aims to curb illegal fishing activities, drug trafficking, and other illicit maritime operations plaguing the shared waters.
• This enhanced cooperation follows recent bilateral discussions focusing on strengthening maritime security and addressing humanitarian concerns of fishermen.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to upholding international maritime law and ensuring the safety and security of the sea lanes.
• The patrols are expected to improve real-time information sharing and operational coordination between the two navies.

GS3.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint patrol at sea.
GS3.trivia: The Palk Strait is a narrow strip of water that separates the Indian state of Tamil Nadu from Sri Lanka, connecting the Bay of Bengal in the northeast with the Gulf of Mannar in the southwest.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils National AI Strategy Framework for Ethical Development and Deployment
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released India's National AI Strategy Framework, focusing on ethical AI development, data governance, and fostering innovation.
• The framework outlines guidelines for responsible AI, emphasizing principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy in AI systems.
• It proposes establishing a National AI Council and sector-specific AI centers of excellence to drive research and application across various domains.
• The strategy aims to position India as a global hub for AI innovation while ensuring that AI benefits all sections of society and addresses national priorities.
• Key focus areas include AI for healthcare, agriculture, smart cities, and education, alongside skill development programs for the AI workforce.

GS3.trivia: India's first national strategy for AI, 'AI for All', was released by NITI Aayog in 2018, focusing on leveraging AI for social good.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase II Shows Promising Results in River Clean-up and Ecosystem Restoration
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti reported significant progress in the second phase of the Namami Gange Mission, focusing on comprehensive river basin management.
• Achievements include the commissioning of several new sewage treatment plants (STPs), reduction in industrial pollution discharge, and enhanced ecological flow in the Ganges.
• Efforts have also been made in afforestation along the river banks, promoting organic farming in the basin, and community engagement for river conservation.
• Challenges remain in ensuring continuous operation and maintenance of infrastructure, managing non-point source pollution, and behavioral change among communities.
• The mission aims for 'Aviral and Nirmal Ganga' (uninterrupted and clean Ganga) by 2027, integrating conservation with economic development.

GS3.headline: Cabinet Approves New PLI Scheme to Boost Advanced Textile Manufacturing and Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for the advanced textile sector, including technical textiles and MMF (Man-Made Fibre) apparel.
• The scheme aims to attract significant investment, create employment, and enhance India's manufacturing capabilities in high-value textile products.
• Incentives will be provided for eligible manufacturers based on incremental sales and investment in specified product segments.
• This move is designed to make India globally competitive in textile manufacturing, reduce import dependence, and boost exports.
• The textile sector is a major employer, and this scheme is expected to drive modernization and technological upgrades, contributing to economic growth.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Sustainable Tourism for Future' Policy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled its 'Sustainable Tourism for Future' policy, aiming to promote environmentally responsible and culturally sensitive tourism practices across India.
• The policy focuses on minimizing the negative environmental impact of tourism, preserving local cultures, and ensuring economic benefits for host communities.
• It includes guidelines for eco-tourism development, waste management in tourist destinations, promotion of local handicrafts, and responsible wildlife tourism.
• Incentives will be provided for tourism operators and states adopting sustainable certification standards and green technologies.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to the UN Sustainable Development Goals and seeks to reposition India as a leading sustainable tourism destination globally.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in the Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• The newly identified amphibian, characterized by unique vocalizations and distinct morphological features, highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region.
• The discovery underscores the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from habitat loss and climate change.
• Researchers employed advanced genetic sequencing and bioacoustics to confirm the species' novelty, adding to the growing list of endemic species in the region.
• This finding is expected to bolster ecological studies and conservation strategies for amphibians, which are often indicators of environmental health.

GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species, making it one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS3.headline: Government Initiatives Boost Millet Production and Consumption for Enhanced Food Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare highlighted the success of various government initiatives aimed at promoting millet cultivation and consumption across India.
• These initiatives include financial support to farmers, development of drought-resistant millet varieties, and public awareness campaigns about the nutritional benefits of millets.
• The push for millets aligns with the 'International Year of Millets 2023' momentum and India's commitment to diversifying food baskets for better nutrition and climate resilience.
• Millets are recognized for their high nutritional value, low water requirement, and ability to thrive in arid conditions, making them ideal for sustainable agriculture.
• Efforts are underway to integrate millets into public distribution systems and mid-day meal schemes to combat malnutrition.
